news.title: DEM Parti'nin çerçeve yasa beklentisi: İtirazlar ve talepler neler?
news.summary: İktidarın "Terörsüz Türkiye" olarak adlandırdığı yeni çözüm sürecinin hukuki altyapısının ilk adımı olacak çerçeve yasaya ilişkin tartışmalar sürüyor. DEM Partililerle görüşen Ayşe Sayın Ankara'dan bildiriyor.

news.title: İsrail, yeni imzalanan çerçeve anlaşması uyarınca Lübnan'daki iki bölgeden çekilecek
news.summary: İsrail ve Lübnan, ABD arabuluculuğunda birkaç gün süren müzakerelerin ardından 26 Haziran'da Washington'da bir çerçeve anlaşması imzaladı.

news.title: ABD, bir yük gemisine saldırının ardından İran'ı vurdu
news.summary: ABD Başkan Donald Trump İran'ı, Hürmüz Boğazı'nda bir yük gemisine düzenlenen saldırının ardından ateşkesi "akılsızca ihlal etmekle" suçladı. Ardından ABD ordusu İran hedeflerine saldırılar düzenledi.

news.title: Deniz Göktaş'ın gösterisinden kesitlerin bulunduğu X paylaşımlarına erişim engeli
news.summary: Komedyen Deniz Göktaş'ın son stad-up gösterisi Ölü Deniz'den X'te paylaşılan iki kesit Türkiye'den görünmez kılındı.

news.title: 'Sperm kardeşleri': Aynı sperm bağışçısından doğduklarını 20'lerinde fark ettiler
news.summary: Natasha, Gemma ve Helen birbirlerini bulmanın "bir peri masalı" gibi olduğunu söylüyor.

news.title: İngiltere'de başbakanlar neden sürekli değişiyor?
news.summary: Keir Starmer, son 10 yılda görevden ayrılan altıncı İngiltere başbakanı oldu. BBC'den Rob Watson, bu istikrarsızlığın ardındaki nedenleri inceliyor.

news.title: Göç İdaresi Başkanlığı Türkiye'deki Suriyeli sayısını açıkladı

news.title: Ömer Çelik: Terörsüz Türkiye sürecinde yeni aşamaya geçtik

news.title: ABP Genel Başkanı'nın iddiası: "CHP'den 86 vekil bize katılacak"
news.summary: Anadolu Birliği Partisi Genel Başkanı Bedri Yalçın, mutlak butlan kararı sonrası CHP Grup Başkanı Özgür Özel ve ekibiyle görüşmeler yürüttüğünü, 86 vekilin ABP'ye katılacağını ileri sürdü.

news.title: Adalar Belediyesi'nde başkanvekili belli oldu

news.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive
news.summary: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news.

news.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
news.summary: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

news.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
news.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

news.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
news.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

news.title: Europe's deadly heatwave breaks German record and halts public events
news.summary: Germany's highest ever temperature of 41.3C is recorded provisionally in Saarbrücken, over the border from France.

news.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
news.summary: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

news.title: With water cuts looming in Arizona in US, locals fight data centres
news.summary: Arizona residents have campaigned against state’s data centres as they seek a share of the shrinking water supply.

news.title: Sabalenka defends Wimbledon prize protest, says it’s for struggling players
news.summary: The world number one is in a group of players seeking a greater prize return at Grand Slams.

news.title: Bodycam shows driver offering armed police a lift mid-chase
news.summary: Bodycam video showing a van driver offering an armed officer a lift to catch a fleeing suspect has gone viral in the UK.

news.title: Toxic Bielsa leaves ‘nothing good’ behind as Uruguay suffer World Cup shock
news.summary: Uruguay's outgoing Argentinian coach Marcelo Bielsa has previously described himself as a 'toxic perfectionist'.

news.title: Cape Verde break record as smallest nation to reach World Cup knockouts
news.summary: Cape Verde set sights on Lionel Messi's Argentina in last 32 as dream run continues at 2026 edition of FIFA's showpiece.

news.title: Israel-Lebanon deal ties ceasefire to Hezbollah disarmament: Will it work?

news.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
news.summary: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

news.title: Opinion: Ranch dressing is a winner at the World Cup games
news.summary: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the popularity of ranch dressing among international visitors to the U.S. during the World Cup games.

news.title: Trump administration partially lifts export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model
news.summary: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.

news.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
news.summary: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?

news.title: How coach Mauricio Pochettino made believers out of the U.S. World Cup team
news.summary: Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired. His rebuild was bumpy at times — but now, with the U.S. headed to the World Cup knockout stage, the players are all in.

news.title: As Supreme Court expands Trump's immigration power, experts warn of steeper U.S. population decline
news.summary: The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.

facts.text: Honey never spoils — edible honey has been found in ancient tombs.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Bananas are berries; strawberries are not.
